key_insight: PPI-GD reduces gradient oracle calls by estimating full gradients from interpolated first-order samples in the data domain, yielding better oracle complexity when the loss is smooth in data.
engineering_relevance: This could lower training cost for large ERM problems by making gradient-based optimization cheaper when data dimension is moderate and smoothness assumptions hold.
method: Query the first-order oracle at equidistant points in the data domain.
Build piecewise tensor-product polynomial interpolants over patches of the data space.
Use the interpolated gradient estimate inside gradient descent for strongly convex and non-convex objectives.
evidence_signal: The paper provides an oracle-complexity analysis, comparing PPI-GD against several GD variants in regimes where the loss is sufficiently smooth and the data dimension is polylogarithmic in the number of samples.
watch_item: The method depends on strong smoothness assumptions and on data-space dimension staying only polylogarithmic in the sample size, which may limit practical scalability.
paper_title: On the Oracle Complexity of Interpolation-Based Gradient Descent
